response:
Task  
The hexagonal community center is a regular hexagon with side length 30 meters. The area of a regular hexagon can be calculated using the formula:  
$$ \text{Area} = \frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2} \times \text{side}^2 $$  
Substituting the side length of 30 meters, the area of the hexagon is:  
$$ \text{Area} = \frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2} \times 30^2 = \frac{3\sqrt{3}}{2} \times 900 = 1350\sqrt{3} \text{ square meters} $$  

Task  
The circular garden is inscribed within the hexagon, with its circumference touching the midpoints of the hexagon's sides. The radius of the circle can be determined by finding the distance from the center of the hexagon to the midpoint of one of its sides. This distance is equal to the apothem of the hexagon. The apothem of a regular hexagon is given by:  
$$ \text{Apothem} = \frac{s\sqrt{3}}{2} $$  
where $ s $ is the side length. Substituting $ s = 30 $, the apothem is:  
$$ \text{Apothem} = \frac{30\sqrt{3}}{2} = 15\sqrt{3} \text{ meters} $$  
The radius of the circular garden is equal to the apothem, so the area of the garden is:  
$$ \text{Area} = \pi \times (15\sqrt{3})^2 = \pi \times 675 = 675\pi \text{ square meters} $$
